examples.title: Algorithm: Queue Operations Pseudocode
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating basic queue operations including enqueue, dequeue, peek, isEmpty, and isFull for both linear and circular queue implementations
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LinearQueueOperations
// Linear Queue using Array Implementation

STRUCTURE LinearQueue
 DATA: queue[0..MAX-1]
 front : INTEGER
 rear : INTEGER
 MAX : INTEGER (capacity)
ENDSTRUCTURE

PROCEDURE initQueue(VAR q : LinearQueue)
 q.front ← -1
 q.rear ← -1
 q.MAX ← MAX_SIZE
ENDPROCEDURE

FUNCTION isEmpty(q : LinearQueue) : BOOLEAN
 RETURN (q.front = -1 OR q.front > q.rear)
ENDFUNCTION

FUNCTION isFull(q : LinearQueue) : BOOLEAN
 RETURN (q.rear = q.MAX - 1)
ENDFUNCTION

PROCEDURE enqueue(VAR q : LinearQueue, item : DATA)
 IF isFull(q) THEN
 PRINT "Queue Overflow!"
 RETURN
 ENDIF
 
 IF isEmpty(q) THEN
 q.front ← 0
 ENDIF
 
 q.rear ← q.rear + 1
 q.queue[q.rear] ← item
 PRINT item + " enqueued successfully"
ENDPROCEDURE

FUNCTION dequeue(VAR q : LinearQueue) : DATA
 IF isEmpty(q) THEN
 PRINT "Queue Underflow!"
 RETURN NULL
 ENDIF
 
 DATA item ← q.queue[q.front]
 q.front ← q.front + 1
 
 IF q.front > q.rear THEN
 // Reset queue when empty
 q.front ← -1
 q.rear ← -1
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN item
ENDFUNCTION

FUNCTION peek(q : LinearQueue) : DATA
 IF isEmpty(q) THEN
 PRINT "Queue is Empty!"
 RETURN NULL
 ENDIF
 RETURN q.queue[q.front]
ENDFUNCTION

// =============================================
// CIRCULAR QUEUE OPERATIONS
// =============================================

ALGORITHM CircularQueueOperations

PROCEDURE initCircularQueue(VAR q : LinearQueue)
 q.front ← -1
 q.rear ← -1
ENDPROCEDURE

FUNCTION isFullCircular(q : LinearQueue) : BOOLEAN
 RETURN ((q.rear + 1) MOD q.MAX) = q.front
ENDFUNCTION

PROCEDURE enqueueCircular(VAR q : LinearQueue, item : DATA)
 IF isFullCircular(q) THEN
 PRINT "Queue is Full!"
 RETURN
 ENDIF
 
 IF q.front = -1 THEN
 q.front ← 0
 ENDIF
 
 q.rear ← (q.rear + 1) MOD q.MAX
 q.queue[q.rear] ← item
ENDPROCEDURE

FUNCTION dequeueCircular(VAR q : LinearQueue) : DATA
 IF q.front = -1 THEN
 PRINT "Queue is Empty!"
 RETURN NULL
 ENDIF
 
 DATA item ← q.queue[q.front]
 
 IF q.front = q.rear THEN
 // Queue becomes empty after this dequeue
 q.front ← -1
 q.rear ← -1
 ELSE
 q.front ← (q.front + 1) MOD q.MAX
 ENDIF
 
 RETURN item
ENDFUNCTION
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates both linear and circular queue operations. Key operations include: isEmpty checks if front is -1 or front > rear; isFull checks if rear equals MAX-1 for linear or (rear+1)%MAX equals front for circular. Enqueue increments rear and inserts element; dequeue retrieves from front and increments front pointer. Circular queue uses modulo arithmetic to wrap around the array, solving the memory wastage problem of linear queues.

examples.title: Python: Queue Implementation with Operations
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of queue operations using both list-based and circular queue approaches
examples.code:
"""
Queue Operations Implementation in Python
Demonstrates Linear Queue and Circular Queue with all basic operations
"""

class LinearQueue:
 """Linear Queue implementation using Python list"""
 
 def __init__(self, capacity=5):
 """Initialize queue with given capacity"""
 self.capacity = capacity
 self.queue = [None] * capacity
 self.front = -1
 self.rear = -1
 
 def is_empty(self):
 """Check if queue is empty"""
 return self.front == -1 or self.front > self.rear
 
 def is_full(self):
 """Check if queue is full"""
 return self.rear == self.capacity - 1
 
 def enqueue(self, item):
 """Add element to rear of queue - O(1)"""
 if self.is_full():
 print(f"Queue Overflow! Cannot enqueue {item}")
 return False
 
 # First element being added
 if self.is_empty():
 self.front = 0
 
 self.rear += 1
 self.queue[self.rear] = item
 print(f"{item} enqueued to queue")
 return True
 
 def dequeue(self):
 """Remove and return element from front - O(1)"""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Queue Underflow! Queue is empty")
 return None
 
 item = self.queue[self.front]
 self.front += 1
 
 # Reset queue when it becomes empty
 if self.front > self.rear:
 self.front = -1
 self.rear = -1
 
 print(f"{item} dequeued from queue")
 return item
 
 def peek(self):
 """Return front element without removing - O(1)"""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Queue is empty")
 return None
 return self.queue[self.front]
 
 def size(self):
 """Return current number of elements - O(1)"""
 if self.is_empty():
 return 0
 return self.rear - self.front + 1
 
 def display(self):
 """Display queue contents"""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Queue is empty")
 return
 print(f"Queue: {self.queue[self.front:self.rear+1]}")
 print(f"Front={self.front}, Rear={self.rear}")


class CircularQueue:
 """Circular Queue implementation - solves memory wastage issue"""
 
 def __init__(self, capacity=5):
 self.capacity = capacity
 self.queue = [None] * capacity
 self.front = -1
 self.rear = -1
 
 def is_empty(self):
 """Check if queue is empty"""
 return self.front == -1
 
 def is_full(self):
 """Check if queue is full"""
 return (self.rear + 1) % self.capacity == self.front
 
 def enqueue(self, item):
 """Add element using circular increment - O(1)"""
 if self.is_full():
 print(f"Queue is Full! Cannot enqueue {item}")
 return False
 
 # First element
 if self.front == -1:
 self.front = 0
 
 self.rear = (self.rear + 1) % self.capacity
 self.queue[self.rear] = item
 print(f"{item} enqueued to circular queue")
 return True
 
 def dequeue(self):
 """Remove element using circular increment - O(1)"""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Queue is Empty!")
 return None
 
 item = self.queue[self.front]
 
 # Last element being removed
 if self.front == self.rear:
 self.front = -1
 self.rear = -1
 else:
 self.front = (self.front + 1) % self.capacity
 
 print(f"{item} dequeued from circular queue")
 return item
 
 def peek(self):
 """Return front element without removing"""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Queue is empty")
 return None
 return self.queue[self.front]
 
 def size(self):
 """Calculate size in circular queue"""
 if self.is_empty():
 return 0
 if self.rear >= self.front:
 return self.rear - self.front + 1
 return self.capacity - self.front + self.rear + 1
 
 def display(self):
 """Display circular queue contents"""
 if self.is_empty():
 print("Queue is empty")
 return
 
 elements = []
 i = self.front
 while True:
 elements.append(self.queue[i])
 if i == self.rear:
 break
 i = (i + 1) % self.capacity
 
 print(f"Queue: {elements}")
 print(f"Front={self.front}, Rear={self.rear}, Size={self.size()}")


# Demonstration
if __name__ == "__main__":
 print("=" * 50)
 print("Linear Queue Demonstration")
 print("=" * 50)
 lq = LinearQueue(5)
 
 lq.enqueue(10)
 lq.enqueue(20)
 lq.enqueue(30)
 lq.display()
 
 lq.dequeue()
 lq.display()
 
 print(f"Peek: {lq.peek()}")
 
 print("\n" + "=" * 50)
 print("Circular Queue Demonstration")
 print("=" * 50)
 cq = CircularQueue(5)
 
 # Demonstrate circular nature
 for i in range(1, 6):
 cq.enqueue(i * 10)
 
 cq.display()
 
 # Dequeue some elements
 cq.dequeue()
 cq.dequeue()
 
 # Enqueue more to show circular wrapping
 cq.enqueue(60)
 cq.enqueue(70)
 
 cq.display()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation shows both linear and circular queue. The LinearQueue class demonstrates the basic FIFO operations but suffers from memory wastage as front advances. The CircularQueue class uses modulo arithmetic to wrap around, efficiently reusing array spaces. All operations (enqueue, dequeue, peek, isEmpty, isFull) execute in O(1) constant time. The display methods help visualize queue contents and pointer positions.

examples.title: C: Queue Operations Implementation
examples.description: Complete C implementation of queue operations demonstrating both linear and circular queue with proper memory management and error handling
examples.explanation: This C implementation provides complete queue operations for both linear and circular queues. The linear queue demonstrates the 'false overflow' problem where the queue appears full even after removing elements. The circular queue solves this using modulo arithmetic: enqueue uses rear = (rear + 1) % capacity and dequeue uses front = (front + 1) % capacity. All operations are O(1) constant time. The demonstration in main shows both implementations and highlights the linear queue limitation.

examples.title: Java: Queue Operations with Generics
examples.description: Object-oriented Java implementation of queue operations using generics and demonstrating both array-based and linked-list based implementations
examples.explanation: This Java implementation demonstrates queue operations using generics for type safety. The ArrayQueue class implements a circular queue using modulo arithmetic for efficient space utilization. The LinkedQueue class uses a linked list approach which dynamically grows and doesn't have a fixed capacity. Both implementations provide O(1) enqueue and dequeue operations. The Java version uses exceptions for overflow/underflow conditions and demonstrates the use of generics to create type-safe queues for any data type.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for all operations in both implementations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for array-based, O(n) for linked list based
